title: Outdoor Environmental Education - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Outdoor Environmental Education 8 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BIRD ALLIANCE OF OREGON EDUCATION PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers environmental education for all ages through trips, classes, camps, mentorship, school programs, and Outdoor School For All. 5151 NW Cornell Road Portland, OR 97210 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by program Email (503) 292-6855 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CAMP TAMARACK SUMMER CAMP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a camp with a range of options such as T-shirt printing, ceramics, paddleboarding, snorkeling, sports, and camping. The schedule changes regularly and may include crafts, cooking, archery, scavenger hunts, canoeing, hiking, and water activities. 19696 Sunshine Way Bend, OR 97702 Eligibility: Ages 8-12 Hours: Camps are 6 days long and run weekly from June through August. Registration opens in January. Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT OUTDOOR SCHOOL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates outdoor school and other environmental education services. Sends 6th-grade students to outdoor school and involves high school students as student leaders. 11611 NE Ainsworth Circle Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Students in grades K-12 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 257-1600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEARBY NATURE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Staff and volunteers lead nature walks, host school programs, sponsor special events, teach both in-person and virtual youth programs, and help with environmental restoration projects. Nearby Nature also maintains an educational Learnscape Garden as well. 622 Day Island Road Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email (541) 687-9699 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEXT DOOR SUMMER CAMPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of engaging camps to help local youth stay active and entertained all summer. Camp directors and counselors ensure participants enjoy their summer while feeling safe and supported in new experiences. 965 Tucker Road Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: 1st - 8th Grade Registration closed for 2026 summer program Hours: Entering 6th-8th Grade Camp: July 2, 2026 - August 13, 2026; 9am-2pm Thursdays Entering 1st-5th Grade Camp: June 29, 2026 - August 12, 2026; 8:30am-2pm Monday-Wednesday Email (541) 386-6665 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON MUSEUM OF SCIENCE AND INDUSTRY SUMMER CAMPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers day classes in the Portland Metro area, overnight camps at the coast and in the desert, and backpacking trips in the Wallowas and Three Sisters Wilderness. Also offers Family Camps over Memorial and Labor Day weekends. Camps and classes vary in length, from 4 – 12 days. 1945 SE Water Avenue Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Varies by course or camp Hours: Varies by course or camp Email (503) 797-4000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND PARKS AND RECREATION CITY OF PORTLAND PARKS AND RECREATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides cultural, athletic, fitness (youth and adult sports), dance, music, science, language, and swimming. Parent and child activity groups are available. Drop-in sports schedule and indoor park schedule on website. Offers a variety of outdoor education that focuses on environmental stewardship and nature connection, including field trips, summer camps, and guided walks. Volunteer opportunities are available. Operates and maintains city parks, gardens, racquet centers, golf courses, natural areas, hiking trails, the Portland International Raceway, and community centers. 1120 SW 5th Avenue Suite 858 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Unrestricted. Activities and classes available for all ages, with special programs for young children, youth, older adults, and adults with disabilities. Hours: Office hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Hours vary at recreation sites Email (503) 823-7529 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TWIN ROCKS FRIENDS CAMP & CONFERENCE CENTER SUMMER CAMP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides youth with active, adventure-filled experiences in a safe and caring environment. Camps include outdoor activities, creative time outside, positive friendships, and faith-based teaching and guidance. 18705 Highway 101 N Rockaway Beach, OR 97136 Eligibility: Grades 1-12 Hours: Monday, June 28, 2026-Saturday, August 15, 2026 Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details BIRD ALLIANCE OF OREGON EDUCATION PROGRAMS CAMP TAMARACK SUMMER CAMP MULTNOMAH EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT OUTDOOR SCHOOL NEARBY NATURE NEARBY NATURE NEXT DOOR SUMMER CAMPS OREGON MUSEUM OF SCIENCE AND INDUSTRY SUMMER CAMPS PORTLAND PARKS AND RECREATION CITY OF PORTLAND PARKS AND RECREATION TWIN ROCKS FRIENDS CAMP & CONFERENCE CENTER SUMMER CAMP
